title: hieracium permaculatum subsp. permaculatum
intro: Hieracium permaculatum subsp. permaculatum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal compounds.
